task_completion.description: Open the http.cap file in a network protocol analyzer like Wireshark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wireshark (or a similar network analysis tool) is open, and the interface or title bar clearly indicates that '/home/ga/Documents/captures/http.cap' is loaded.
The text 'http' is typed into the display filter toolbar at the top of the Wireshark interface.

task_completion.description: Apply the HTTP filter to isolate the traffic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The display filter is active (indicated by a green background in the Wireshark filter bar) and the packet list view updates to display only HTTP protocol packets.

task_completion.description: Initiate the export of the filtered packets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export Specified Packets' dialog box is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Export the resulting traffic to filtered_http.pcap.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows the file being saved exactly as 'filtered_http.pcap' in the '/home/ga/Documents/captures/' directory, or a terminal/file manager window confirms the new file exists in that specific location.

integrity.description: Verify the file was exported from the analyzer and not just created as an empty/fake file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the 'Export Specified Packets' feature in Wireshark (or using a valid tshark command), rather than using 'touch' or a text editor to artificially create 'filtered_http.pcap'.

integrity.description: Verify that only the filtered (displayed) packets are being exported, not the entire capture.
integrity.visual_evidence: In the Wireshark export dialog, the 'Displayed' radio button/toggle must be selected in the Packet Range section, ensuring the filter is actually applied to the exported file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't simply copy the original file to the new name.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen running 'cp http.cap filtered_http.pcap' or using a file manager to duplicate the file. The process must involve actual protocol filtering.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source file is 'http.cap' (26KB, 43 total packets). The correct Wireshark display filter to isolate HTTP traffic is exactly 'http'. The expected output file must be named 'filtered_http.pcap' and saved in '/home/ga/Documents/captures/'.
